name: Big loop starting from Tarbes
name_original: Grande boucle à partir de Tarbes
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tarbes, located at the foot of the Pyrenees, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features challenging mountain passes leading into the Pyrenees National Park, alongside green valleys and dedicated cycling paths like the "Chemin Vert" and "Voie Verte des Gaves." Routes follow rivers such as the Adour and Echez, providing a mix of terrain from demanding ascents to gentler rides through the Pyrenean foothills. Best touring cycling routes around Tarbes The most popular touring cycling route is Pyrenean Piedmont: Tarbes and Lourdes Loop, a 41.8 miles (67.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route connects Tarbes with Lourdes, traversing the Pyrenean Piedmont. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Big loop starting from Tarbes, a difficult 45.9 miles (73.9 km) path. This extensive loop offers views of the surrounding Pyrenean foothills. Local touring cyclists also love the On the banks of the Adour – Lac de Bours loop from Tarbes, a 10.8 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through riverbanks and past Lac de Bours, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Tarbes is defined by Pyrenean foothills, green valleys, and dedicated cycling paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides along rivers to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Tarbes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 2900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tarbes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Tarbes?
A: Tarbes, situated at the foot of the Pyrenees, offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find challenging mountain passes leading into the Pyrenees National Park, as well as gentler rides through green valleys and along rivers like the Adour and Echez. The Pyrenean foothills provide varied experiences on quiet country roads and through small villages.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Tarbes?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35690275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Adour – Lac de Bours loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 10.8-mile (17.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 5 minutes, following riverbanks and passing Lac de Bours. Another easy option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38539668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Adour – Lac de Bours loop from Clairière&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21 km long.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes near Tarbes?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes that tackle legendary Tour de France climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861755581/big-loop-starting-from-tarbes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Big loop starting from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.9-mile (73.9 km) path with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41695013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aspin – Col d'Aspin West Approach loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 96.3 km and includes significant ascents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around Tarbes offer views of the majestic Pyrenees and pass by various natural and cultural landmarks. You can explore areas around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5606547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrêt-Darré Lake&lt;/a&gt; or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Sever-de-Rustan Abbey&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also traverse picturesque valleys and offer glimpses of the Pyrenees National Park.

Q: Is it possible to cycle from Tarbes to Lourdes?
A: Yes, a popular route connects Tarbes with Lourdes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e862565793/pyrenean-piedmont-tarbes-and-lourdes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pyrenean Piedmont: Tarbes and Lourdes Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 41.8-mile (67.2 km) trail that takes approximately 4 hours 28 minutes to complete, traversing the Pyrenean Piedmont between the two cities. This route often follows dedicated cycling paths like the "Chemin Vert" and "Voie Verte des Gaves."

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tarbes?
A: The best time for touring cycling around Tarbes is generally from spring to autumn. During these seasons, the weather is more favorable, and the mountain passes are typically clear of snow, allowing access to the higher Pyrenean routes. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide cooler rides and beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available from Tarbes?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Tarbes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861755581/big-loop-starting-from-tarbes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Big loop starting from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35690275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Adour – Lac de Bours loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28966580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarbes – Tarbes Cathedral loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 50 km ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Tarbes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tarbes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic rides along rivers, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or rivers?
A: Absolutely. The region features several routes that follow riverbanks and pass by lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35690275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Adour – Lac de Bours loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you along the Adour river and past Lac de Bours. You can also find routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5606547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrêt-Darré Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic viewpoints of the Pyrenees?
A: Many routes venturing into the Pyrenean foothills or higher mountain passes offer breathtaking panoramas. The region is known for its stunning views, especially from iconic climbs like Col d'Aspin. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41704465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chemin des Palombes – Scenic Road Through Campan loop from Tournay&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides extensive views as it covers nearly 100 km with significant elevation.

Q: Are there dedicated cycling paths in and around Tarbes?
A: Yes, Tarbes and its surroundings benefit from dedicated cycling infrastructure. Within the city, there are cycle paths for exploring. Beyond the city, you can utilize the "Chemin Vert" which links Tarbes to Lourdes, and the "Voie Verte des Gaves" connecting Lourdes to Pierrefitte-Nestalas, offering accessible and scenic routes through picturesque valleys.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Tarbes area?
A: There are over 470 touring cycling routes available in the Tarbes area, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 90 easy routes, 190 moderate routes, and nearly 200 difficult routes, ensuring options for every type of cyclist.
